what_it_does: Registers a single MCP tool named create that authors new declarative registry entries. It derives the allowed kinds from the Suite's create_<kind> methods at registration, validates the request, dispatches to the matching Suite method, and returns the result along with the drift-home reflection path.
observations: Consolidates eight prior create_* tools into one tool selected by a kind argument.
Valid kinds are discovered from callable Suite methods named create_*, excluding create_node.
A _DRIFT_HOME dict maps each declarative kind to a corresponding AGENTS.md file.
The tool requires a spec dict containing an id and accepts an optional model argument only for kind='role'.
It dispatches via getattr(suite, f'create_{kind}') and passes model only when kind is role.
The response is enriched with kind, reflected_in, and reflected: True.
The docstring encodes design principles such as registry-is-truth, declarative-direct, and a floor that gates executable-code/node creation.
standouts: The kind enum is derived live from Suite methods rather than hardcoded.
Drift-home reflection is reported automatically in the tool response.
Explicit floor: graph nodes and executable code are kept off this tool and routed through node(op='create').
The inner _kinds() recomputes the kind list at call time, while KindT is fixed at registration.
conventions: Follows the MCP tool registration pattern with @mcp.tool().
Uses getattr for dynamic method dispatch by constructed name.
Uses typing.Literal for the kind parameter type.
Uses a long docstring as both design documentation and user-facing tool description.
concerns: The local variable _kinds (a tuple) is shadowed by the function _kinds(), making the tuple unused and potentially confusing.
KindT is built once at registration, but _kinds() recomputes inside the tool, so a newly added Suite method would be accepted at runtime before the type annotation or server restart catches up.
The tool checks that spec has an id but does not validate that id is a legal Python identifier.
It assumes every suite.create_<kind> accepts the expected arguments and returns a dict-like result without introspecting signatures.
notes: This is the consolidated CREATE surface for declarative registry entries: it delegates to the Suite and reports both the created entry and where its drift-home reflection lives.
questions: Why is the _kinds tuple shadowed by a _kinds function of the same name?
How is the id validated as a legal identifier before file creation?
Where exactly does the tempdir gating and commit happen — inside each Suite.create_<kind>?
What happens if a Suite.create_<kind> method returns a non-dict value?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a single declarative-authoring tool that dispatches to Suite.create_<kind>, derives kinds from the suite, and reports reflection; the file does exactly that and matches its purpose.
apparent_themes: Consolidation and reuse over parallel tools
intention_signals: The file is trying to provide a unified, self-documenting, safe authoring interface where the set of creatable registry kinds is discovered from the Suite rather than hardcoded, and where each creation is automatically reflected into a canonical drift-home document for self-teaching.
novelty: The live derivation of the tool's kind enum from Suite method names, combined with explicit exclusion of graph-node creation and automatic reporting of the drift-home reflection path, is a distinctive design choice.
contribution: A single create tool that dynamically discovers declarative registry kinds from the Suite, dispatches to the appropriate Suite.create_<kind>, and surfaces both the created entry and its drift-home reflection location.
summary_for_embedding: mcp_face/tools/create.py registers a consolidated MCP create tool that authors declarative registry entries by dispatching to Suite.create_<kind> methods, deriving valid kinds at registration and reporting automatic drift-home reflection.
intention_for_embedding: To give agents one neutral, live, correctness-gated authoring surface for declarative registry entries while keeping executable-code and graph-node creation gated elsewhere.

symbols.does: Registers the create MCP tool by deriving kinds from the Suite and returning the decorated create function.
symbols.description: Closes over the mcp and suite arguments, computes an initial kind tuple, builds a KindT literal, defines the inner _kinds helper, and returns the create tool function decorated with @mcp.tool().

symbols.does: Recomputes the list of allowed declarative kinds from the Suite at call time.
symbols.description: Inspects dir(suite) for callable methods starting with create_, strips the prefix, and excludes create_node. It is used inside create to report the current valid kinds when validation fails.

symbols.does: MCP tool implementation that validates input and dispatches to suite.create_<kind>.
symbols.description: Validates that kind is in the live kind list and that spec is a dict with an id, calls the matching Suite method (passing model only for roles), then enriches the returned result with kind, reflected_in from _DRIFT_HOME, and reflected: True.
